changed_functions.name: AtapiSendAtaIdentify (sub_1C00020C0)
key_changes: Register reallocation between unpatched and patched versions (e.g., rsi/rbp/rbx role swaps)
IDENTIFY command dispatch logic is semantically identical between versions

changed_functions.name: AtapiHandleMiniportCommand (sub_1C000272C)
key_changes: Control flow restructuring in the miniport request handler — if-else branch ordering changed but logic is equivalent
Register assignments differ but all paths compute the same results

changed_functions.name: AtapiProcessInterrupt (sub_1C0002AA0)
key_changes: Byte-load optimization: unpatched uses 'mov al, byte [mem]' at 0x1c0002c25 then 'movzx eax, al' before multiply; patched uses 'movzx eax, byte [mem]' at load point — functionally identical since unpatched zero-extends before any computation
Bit-test encoding: unpatched uses 'mov ecx, 0x200; test word [mem], cx' at 0x1c0002ada; patched uses 'bt word [mem], 9' — same semantics
The 'remaining < count' clamp (if v12 < v15, v15 = v12) that bounds the port-buffer copy to the residual DataTransferLength is present and identical in both versions
Register reallocation throughout the interrupt data-transfer handler

changed_functions.name: WaitOnBusyUntil (sub_1C00012C8)
key_changes: Wait-loop break handling restructured — different branch layout but identical loop semantics
Register assignment differences only

changed_functions.name: AtapiInitializeDevice (sub_1C0001D64)
key_changes: Loop counter tracking changed from 'rax += 1; jne' to 'rax_2 = r8; r8 += 1; jne' — same iteration semantics
Register reallocation in mode setup function

changed_functions.name: AtapiNoDeviceConnected (sub_1C0001FC8)
key_changes: Status 0xFF early-return check moved from both callers (AtapiSendAtaCommand, AtapiSendAtaIdentify) into this callee in the patched version
Refactoring — both call sites carried the 0xFF check inline in the unpatched build, so centralizing it in the callee preserves identical behavior for both callers

changed_functions.name: AtapiSendAtapiCommand (sub_1C0002500)
key_changes: Load-and-test fold: unpatched uses 'mov al, [rcx+rdi+0xB4]' at 0x1c0002624 followed by 'test al, 2'; patched folds both into 'test byte ptr [rcx+rdi+0xB4], 2' at 0x1c0002638 — same flag result, no multiply involved

The 'DataTransferLength >= 0x10000' clamp to 0xFFFE before writing the ATAPI byte-count registers is present and identical in both versions (unpatched 'cmp ebp, 10000h' / 'mov ebp, 0FFFEh' at 0x1c00025c6; patched 'cmp dword ptr [rbx+18h], 10000h' / 'mov ebp, 0FFFEh' at 0x1c00025d3)
Register reallocation in the ATAPI command send path

changed_functions.name: AtapiSendAtaCommand (sub_1C0002200)
key_changes: Byte-load optimization in the ATA command send / PIO write path: unpatched uses 'mov al, byte [rcx+rdi+0xb]' at 0x1c00023a3 followed by 'movzx eax, al' at 0x1c00023b4 before 'imul esi, eax' at 0x1c00023b7; patched uses 'movzx eax, byte [rcx+rdi+0xb]' at 0x1c00023d0 before 'imul esi, eax' at 0x1c00023e2 — both produce identical zero-extended results before multiply
The 'remaining < count' transfer-size clamp (if v18 < v21, v21 = remaining) bounding the port-buffer write is present and identical in both versions
Register reallocation throughout the ATA command send path

changed_functions.name: AtapiResetAtapiDevices (sub_1C00039C0)
key_changes: Register reallocation in the ATAPI device reset routine
No behavioral changes detected

changed_functions.name: AtapiHwStartIo (sub_1C0001700)
key_changes: IRB dispatch entry point: bit-test encoding change from 'mov ecx, 0x200; test word [mem], cx' to 'bt [mem], 9' — identical semantics
No behavioral changes

changed_functions.name: AtapiSetDmaTransferModeAsync (sub_1C0003C80)
key_changes: Loop counter pattern change from 'rax += 1' to 'rax_2 = r8; r8 += 1' — same semantics
Register reallocation in DMA transfer mode setup

changed_functions.name: AtapiHwBuildIo (sub_1C0001690)
key_changes: Bit-test encoding change: 'test word [mem], 0x200' → 'bt [mem], 9' — identical semantics
Build-IO request validation logic unchanged

key_changes: Register reallocation in the asynchronous status-wait routine
No behavioral changes detected

key_changes: Register reallocation in the asynchronous block-size setup routine
No behavioral changes detected

changed_functions.name: AtapiSetupGeometryAsync (sub_1C0003DF0)
key_changes: Register reallocation in the asynchronous geometry setup routine
No behavioral changes detected

changed_functions.name: AtapiSetPioTransferModeAsync (sub_1C0003BD0)
key_changes: Loop counter pattern change and register reallocation in the asynchronous PIO transfer mode setup routine
No behavioral changes detected

changed_functions.name: __GSHandlerCheckCommon (sub_1C0003FFC)
key_changes: Variable type change in the stack-cookie (GS) exception handler helper (different register width for intermediate value)
No behavioral impact

changed_functions.name: AtapiCompleteRequest (sub_1C0002DDC)
key_changes: Bit-test encoding change: 'mov ecx, 0x200; test word [mem], cx' → 'bt [mem], 9' in the request completion handler — identical semantics
No behavioral changes

added.name: _guard_xfg_dispatch_icall_nop
added.note: eXtended Flow Guard (XFG) indirect-call dispatch thunk emitted by the newer toolchain. A control-flow-integrity mitigation stub, not a fix for any specific flaw in the driver logic.

summary: Exhaustive analysis of all 18 changed functions between atapi_unpatched.sys and atapi_patched.sys revealed NO security vulnerability. Every changed function exhibits only compiler-generated differences: register reallocation (e.g., rsi↔rbp, rbx↔r15 swaps), instruction optimization (mov al + movzx eax,al → movzx eax,byte [mem]), bit-test encoding changes (mov+test → bt), and control flow restructuring (loop counter tracking, branch reordering). The most security-relevant candidate — the byte-load zero-extension pattern in the PIO/DMA data transfer size calculation path (AtapiSendAtaCommand @ sub_1C0002200 0x1c00023a3-0x1c00023b7, AtapiProcessInterrupt @ sub_1C0002AA0 0x1c0002c25-0x1c0002c3a) — was conclusively verified as functionally identical in both versions, because the unpatched version always includes a compensating 'movzx eax, al' before the 'imul' instruction, ensuring no stale upper bits reach any computation. The residual-DataTransferLength bounds clamp that limits the port-buffer copy is present and identical in both binaries (AtapiProcessInterrupt: 'if (residual < count) count = residual'; AtapiSendAtaCommand: same), as is the ATAPI 0xFFFE byte-count clamp in AtapiSendAtapiCommand (sub_1C0002500 0x1c00025c6 vs 0x1c00025d3). The only structural difference beyond recompilation noise is a new eXtended Flow Guard dispatch thunk (_guard_xfg_dispatch_icall_nop @ 0x1C00040B0) in the patched build, a control-flow-integrity mitigation stub rather than a targeted fix. The patch appears to be a compiler recompilation with a newer toolchain, not a targeted security fix. No PoC is applicable.
